data.title: Datadog's Q2 revenue rises 28% YoY to $826.76M, beats estimates. Increase in customers to 3,850 with $100K+ ARR. Margin decline noted.
data.description: Datadog (DDOG) posted its quarterly results, with a 28% year-over-year increase in fiscal second-quarter revenue to $826.76 million, surpassing analyst expectations of $790.97 million. The adjusted EPS stood at 46 cents, higher than the analyst consensus of 42 cents. As of June 30, Datadog had 3,850 customers generating annual recurring revenue (ARR) of $100,000 or more, marking a 14% Y/Y rise. The adjusted gross margin dropped by 100 basis points to 81%, while the adjusted operating margin fell by 400 basis points to 20%. Datadog reported cash and equivalents of $3.9 billion as of June 30. Check the full story for more details.

data.title: Mokas Coffee & Eatery prepares for its 20th anniversary with leadership transition as Scott Redler moves to Board Member.
data.description: Scott Redler, an experienced industry professional, has stepped into the role of Board Member to assist in the advancement of brand growth at Mokas Coffee & Eatery. The establishment, situated in SALINA, Kan., has gained recognition for its meticulously crafted coffee, freshly prepared pastries, and delectable breakfast and lunch selections. This move comes on August 7, 2025, and marks a significant phase for the company's development.

data.title: Meters Music becomes the Tampa Bay Buccaneers' official audio partner for the 2025-26 season, launching on Aug. 9 at Raymond James Stadium.
data.description: A new brand partnership is set to begin on August 9 at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida, as Meters Music, renowned for their vintage-style headphones, earbuds, and high-quality gaming audio systems, disclosed a collaboration with the NFL's Tampa Bay Buccaneers. The alliance, announced on August 7, 2025, marks a significant moment for the companies involved.

data.title: Dispensary 33 budtenders have joined Teamsters Local 777, expanding the union's presence in Illinois cannabis industry.
data.description: In a recent development on August 7, 2025, in Chicago, cannabis workers at Dispensary 33 made a significant move by deciding to join Teamsters Local 777. The decision was met with overwhelming support from the workers, marking a remarkable progress for cannabis workers in Illinois. This move highlights the growing presence of the union in the state's cannabis industry, solidifying its position and influence. Jim Glimco expressed his enthusiasm, emphasizing the importance of this decision in furthering the rights and representation of cannabis workers in Illinois. This development underscores the increasing momentum of unionization within the cannabis sector and signifies a positive change for workers in the industry.

data.title: Wicked Weed Brewing gives $10,000 prize to Asheville artist to rebuild studio post-Helene on National IPA Day.
data.description: A prize worth $10,000 has been awarded to assist a resident of Asheville in reconstructing their art studio post-Helene. The official declaration will coincide with National IPA Day on August 7, 2025, in Asheville, North Carolina. Wicked Weed Brewing has disclosed that Matthew Decker, a local artist, is the recipient of this award.

data.title: Becton Dickinson outperforms in Q3 with adjusted EPS of $3.68, sales of $5.51B, and 10.4% revenue increase.
data.description: Becton, Dickinson, and Company (NYSE: BDX) have seen a strong financial performance in the third quarter of 2025. Their stock is trading higher after reporting better-than-expected earnings. They reported adjusted earnings per share of $3.68, beating the consensus of $3.41, and sales of $5.51 billion, beating the consensus of $5.49 billion. Revenues increased by 10.4% as reported, with a 3.0% organic growth. The medical segment BD Medical saw sales increase by 14.4% to $2.93 billion. GAAP and adjusted gross margins also increased. Additionally, the company's operating income rose significantly. This success highlights the company's resilience and adaptability in the healthcare industry as it recovers from global disruptions.

data.title: Dow Jones dropped after Trump's tariff news, Intel sinks. Apple and Nvidia shares up. Live coverage details.
data.description: On Thursday, the Dow Jones Index experienced a downturn following the announcement of new tariffs by Trump. However, amidst this decline, Apple and Nvidia shares saw a rise. The stock market report for today indicates that the Dow relinquished its gains, with Intel suffering a setback after a critical statement made by Trump.

data.title: Dorel Industries amends loan facility, allows access to $20 million liquidity, total ABL facility availability capped at $150 million, $92 million borrowed.
data.description: Dorel Industries Inc. announced an amendment to its asset backed loan facility on August 7, 2025, extending the forbearance period till September 16, 2025. The lenders have agreed to forebear from enforcing their rights due to a default related to financial covenants. Dorel will receive an additional US$20.0 million in liquidity in three tranches under the ABL facility to finance new inventory. The total maximum availability under the ABL facility was reduced to US$150.0 million. As of June 30, 2025, the borrowings outstanding under the ABL facility were approximately US$92.0 million.

data.title: Richmond American Homes opening Seasons at Loma Rica Ranch on August 16 in Grass Valley, CA. License Number: 01842595.
data.description: On August 16, the Seasons at Loma Rica Ranch will be unveiled in Grass Valley, California. The opening is set to be launched by Richmond American Homes of Maryland, Inc., a licensed real estate broker in California with Corporation License Number 01842595.

data.title: Leggett & Platt declares quarterly dividend of $.05 per share for Q3 2025, payable Oct 15 to shareholders on record by Sep 15.
data.description: In Carthage, Missouri on August 7, 2025, it was announced that Leggett & Platt's Board of Directors approved a dividend of $.05 per share for the third quarter of 2025. Shareholders as of September 15, 2025, will receive the dividend on October 15, 2025. For additional details, please refer to Leggett's official communication channels.

data.title: Toni E. Mitchell appointed President of Hanover's Tech & Life Sciences business, focusing on innovation and underwriting.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, in Worcester, Massachusetts, The Hanover Insurance Group, Inc. (NYSE: THG) revealed the selection of Toni E. Mitchell as the new president of its technology and life sciences division. This move highlights the company's dedication to innovation and high quality underwriting.

data.title: Uber beats Q2 expectations with $46.8B gross bookings, $2.1B EBITDA; Stock rises. Analysts maintain ratings, set price targets: $84-110.
data.description: Uber Technologies Inc. (NYSE: UBER) witnessed a slight increase in shares during early Thursday trading following the release of positive second-quarter results. Analysts shared their insights with Wedbush's Scott Devitt maintaining a Neutral rating with a reduced price target of $84, while JPMorgan's Doug Anmuth retained an Overweight rating and a price target of $110. Additionally, Needham's Bernie McTernan reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $109. Wedbush reported Uber's gross bookings at $46.8 billion, marking a 17.0% YoY increase and surpassing the consensus of $46.4 billion. The adjusted EBITDA stood at $2.1 billion with a 16.7% margin, meeting expectations according to Devitt. Uber's management provided guidance for the third quarter, forecasting gross bookings between $48.3 - $49.8 billion. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Parker Hannifin reports record Q4 sales of $5.24B, net income rises 18% to $923M, adjusted earnings reach $992M.
data.description: Stock of Parker Hannifin Corp. (NYSE: PH) surged after announcing Q4 2025 sales of a historic $5.24 billion, surpassing analysts' $5.10 billion prediction. Net income climbed 18% YoY to $923 million ($7.15 per diluted share) from $785 million ($6.01 per share) last year. Adjusted earnings hit $992 million ($7.69/share) against the estimated $7.11. In the Diversified Industrial sector, North America sales fell 6.9% to $2.08 billion, while operating margins improved by 200 basis points to 24.7% or 170 basis points to 26.7% on an adjusted basis. International sales increased by 4.3% to $1.49 billion, with margins rising by 60 basis points to 22.4% or 80 basis points to 24.7% adjusted. Order rates grew by 2%. Illinois Tool Works also saw a record Q2 and raised its outlook.

data.title: Florida abolishes business rent tax, last in the US, in historic move for commercial real estate. Positively impacts economy.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, a significant development occurred in the commercial real estate industry as Florida made history by abolishing its business rent tax. This tax had been the only statewide tax on commercial leases in the country. The removal of this tax is expected to have a beneficial effect on the economy, opening up new opportunities for businesses and potentially stimulating growth. This decision marks a major milestone for the sector and is likely to have a positive impact on businesses operating in Florida.

data.title: SurgiBox gets CE Mark for SurgiField System to boost safe surgery access globally.	rectification for its SurgiField System, a portable, non-sterile field designed for surgical procedures in challenging environments. This accreditation sets the platform for the company’s international growth and sales boost.
data.description: SurgiBox, a medical technology company based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, shared on August 7, 2025, that it has obtained CE Certification. This certification enables the company to expand its presence globally and boost sales significantly. SurgiBox focuses on enhancing access to safe and sterile surgery wherever it is required. The CE Certification marks a critical milestone for the company, signaling its adherence to strict European standards. This achievement is expected to drive sales and pave the way for market expansion.

data.title: Wastewater treatment market booming, share expected to reach 55% by 2024 and projected to hit 80% by 2030, creating innovation opportunities.
data.description: By 2024, the proportion of treated wastewater is expected to surge from 20% to 55%, with a projected increase to 80% by 2030. This growth in the market presents a valuable opportunity for the implementation of advanced technologies and sustainable solutions. The report, dated August 7, 2025, from BCC highlights the potential for sectors involved in wastewater treatment to strategically capitalize on this trend.
